faqs.question: Can I use AI to handle finding relevant source passages inside long documents without losing the core detail?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you choose the tool that matches the input type and then review the output yourself. SciSpace is the best starting point, Gemini is the strongest second choice, and Microsoft Copilot is the safer fallback when you need a different balance of speed and control.

faqs.question: Which AI tool is better when I need source checking?
faqs.answer: Gemini is usually the better choice when source checking matters, while SciSpace is stronger when the job starts with a known set of files or notes.

faqs.question: Is SciSpace better than Gemini for this workflow?
faqs.answer: SciSpace is better when you want the most direct match for the main input; Gemini is better when the workflow changes enough to justify a different tool.

faqs.question: What should I avoid before I share the result?
faqs.answer: Avoid sharing the first draft until you have checked facts, tone, and any missing context. Use Microsoft Copilot as a helper, not a replacement for review.
